heat_index_system.agency: US NOAA / Indian Meteorological Department Adaptations

levels.description: Fatigue is possible with prolonged exposure and activity.
levels.primary_action: Stay hydrated and monitor local weather updates.

levels.description: Sunstroke, muscle cramps, and heat exhaustion are possible with prolonged exposure and/or physical activity.
levels.primary_action: Drink plenty of water, schedule heavy outdoor work for cooler hours, and seek shade.

levels.description: Sunstroke, muscle cramps, and heat exhaustion are likely; heat stroke is possible with prolonged exposure and/or physical activity.
levels.primary_action: Avoid outdoor activities during peak heat hours, dress in lightweight light-colored clothing, and ensure continuous air circulation.

levels.description: Heat stroke or sunstroke is highly imminent with any outdoor exposure.
levels.primary_action: Stay indoors in air-conditioned spaces, completely suspend heavy outdoor activity, and check on vulnerable neighbors.

wet_bulb_globe_temperature_reference.use_case: Used primarily for outdoor workers and military operations to assess heat load in direct sunlight.

thresholds.action: Caution. Limit training / heavy labor for unacclimatized individuals.
Extreme Caution. Limit intense outdoor activity to 45 minutes per hour.
Danger. Suspend physical training and intense outdoor work for unacclimatized individuals; acclimatized individuals limit work to 30 minutes per hour.
Extreme Danger. Suspend all outdoor physical training and non-essential labor.
